author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content:
Actually, the discontinuation of new ship sales wasn't related to economics. We were using ship releases primarily for two things:

Content release while we were building product,Close the economic loop on Faction Fleet earnings,
Separately, we did, and I still do, feel it's important to have inventory online in preparation for a surge in growth. We aren't operating off of the FOMO model of every mint needs to sell out immediately, even though that was largely the dynamic early on. It's actually beneficial to have new inventory on the shelf for growth. 

We stopped selling ships around the first (or maybe second) 426LIVE event last year, because we felt we had sufficient inventory online, and didn't require ship releases to fill the content gap, given interactive product was available.

content: Are there webgl 3D assets completed for more ships than are offered or is that still a bottle neck?

author: Signal
content: I agree that there is no guarantee that SOL price continues moving up. However we do need SOL just to operate on the chain in general so it's not a bad idea to get setup and get SOL at a discounted price.  It also ties into the F2P program as this will offset the cost of fees for "free" players.  I think it's worth looking at not for an "investment" but a necessity to facilitate everything else SOL is required for in the future. We should be price agnostic.

author: Signal
content: Yeah it’s tricky.  Untangling Star Atlas from ATMTA is the trouble.  ATMTA is a company.  That company has equity.  The value for ATMTA and essentially all of its assets that are worth anything, are tied to Star Atlas.  ATMTA sells POLIS, which gives you voting rights for the project known as Star Atlas (pretty much the sole asset of value for ATMTA), owned by ATMTA?  ATMTA holds the majority of POLIS in order to not lose control of Star Atlas (its own property it would seem).  It’s like a knot that keeps folding back into itself.  It’s hard to distinguish one from the other and what rights, protections, and privileges were actually sold when POLIS was issued.

reply.preview: Yeah it’s tricky. Untangling Star Atlas from ATMTA is the trouble. ATMTA is a company. That company has equity. The value for ATMTA and essentially all of its assets that are worth anything, are tied to Star Atlas. ATMTA sells POLIS, which gives you voting rights for the project known as Star Atlas (pretty much the sole asset of value for ATMTA), owned by ATMTA? ATMTA holds the majority of POLIS in order to not lose control of Star Atlas (its own property it would seem). It’s like a knot that keeps folding back into itself. It’s hard to distinguish one from the other and what rights, protections, and privileges were actually sold when POLIS was issued.

author: Odicerous
content:
You guys have great arguments and discussion points, but I think you are front running the game.
Maybe I’m nuts, but I don’t think the devs should tell us anything right now.  I would not.-

author: Odicerous
content:
If it was me, and I’m not a dev, just a dummy, but… I would do the following:
1- Declare all botting illegal.2-Do nothing and observe how botting develops
3- When it gets to a point that the game/test suffers, throw some levers
4-Observe, see what changes, how does it balance
5-Throw some other levers, see if you can improve game play
6-Observe, see what changes
…and so on.
This is a test.  Try stuff.  Be right.  Be wrong. Learn.At what level is botting beneficial?  Harmful?  Unstoppable?  Now is the time to collect the data.
I would NOT make any kind of policy statement that the community can wave at a later date and say “You said you wouldn’t allow XYZ!”
By the time SAGE v.2 comes out, I hope they will have a good understanding of what the policy should look like.  Until then, try stuff.  Change policies.  Break some shit.  Learn.

author: [AEP] Bodhitree
content: All the best games with the largest budgets for anti cheat have their limits. The most advanced banning systems use not only the ip address, but the individual hardware identifiers within the users system. They could also use on chain wallet trackers in the case of on chain movement. Hackers have already figured out how to swap ip, all hardware identifiers, as well as wash wash crypto transactions to make them impossible to track. What’s more there are already applications that automate all this.

author: Signal
content: If you really care about global warming and climate change and saving the earth etc, go vegan or vegetarian.  The bulk of the methane and carbon emissions are just from livestock (Raising livestock for human consumption generates nearly 15% of total global greenhouse gas emissions, which is greater than all the transportation emissions combined).  It also uses nearly 70% of agricultural land which leads to being the major contributor to deforestation, biodiversity loss, and water pollution.

author: Signal
content: We don’t need a whole new energy system, we need people to be slightly more thoughtful about their habits across the board and a lot of this fixes itself.

author: Signal
content: Either way, my two cents.

author: [AEP] Moustacheride
content: In the days of old, the founders of ATMTA gazed upon the state of games in the Web3 world, and saw great opportunity! They got together and conceived a divine plan, which they wrote down into their White Paper: they would build a truly remarkable Unreal Engine 5 experience, that would capture the s  of all who would behold it!  But the task was monumental, and they knew it would take them many years to complete. And so they conceived a smaller, web-only, mission-based game, to keep their followers and disciples entertained and engaged throughout this time of trial and tribulations.  As months ticked away, the web-only game was delayed, and then delayed some more. The founders stopped what they were doing, took a short break, and afterward sat down to discuss the concept with their newly formed, inner circle of trusted lieutenants.  In the second month of the year 2022, a new idea sprang forth! Instead of a temporary web-game, they would create a game with more permanence, a game in glorious 3D that could yet still be contained within a browser window! And the best part of it all: it would allow them to fully reuse the back-end code, as this new game (codenamed SCREAM) would become a trimmed-down version of the Grand Strategy component they were planning for the full game!  But the months continued to tick away once again, and SCREAM did not come to fruition. Near the end of the year, when the whole world was celebrating the annual Breakpoint festival, the team announced the name for this web-game they were building: Star Atlas - Golden Era! But still, there was no game.  The year 2022 turned into 2023, and the founders realized that the front-end of SAGE was nowhere near what it should be. It would require a ton more work to get right. They prepared their loyal followers for more delays, knowing that this would test their faith yet again.  And then, on the 26th of the 4th month of the year 2023, the first glimpse of what one day would become SAGE was released! The founders looked upon it, and saw that it was good. And they named it: Escape Velocity.  All was good in the world again, at least for a few days. Now the crowds were even more eagerly looking forward to the gameplay that was promised with the announcement of SAGE.  Will ATMTA deliver? And when? Only time will tell…

Now the crowds were even more eagerly looking forward to the gameplay that was promised with the announcement of SAGE.  Will ATMTA deliver? And when? Only time will tell…

reply.preview: In the days of old, the founders of ATMTA gazed upon the state of games in the Web3 world, and saw great opportunity! They got together and conceived a divine plan, which they wrote down into their White Paper: they would build a truly remarkable Unreal Engine 5 experience, that would capture the s of all who would behold it! But the task was monumental, and they knew it would take them many years to complete. And so they conceived a smaller, web-only, mission-based game, to keep their followers and disciples entertained and engaged throughout this time of trial and tribulations. As months ticked away, the web-only game was delayed, and then delayed some more. The founders stopped what they were doing, took a short break, and afterward sat down to discuss the concept with their newly formed, inner circle of trusted lieutenants. In the second month of the year 2022, a new idea sprang forth! Instead of a temporary web-game, they would create a game with more permanence, a game in glorious 3D that could yet still be contained within a browser window! And the best part of it all: it would allow them to fully reuse the back-end code, as this new game (codenamed SCREAM) would become a trimmed-down version of the Grand Strategy component they were planning for the full game! But the months continued to tick away once again, and SCREAM did not come to fruition. Near the end of the year, when the whole world was celebrating the annual Breakpoint festival, the team announced the name for this web-game they were building: Star Atlas - Golden Era! But still, there was no game. The year 2022 turned into 2023, and the founders realized that the front-end of SAGE was nowhere near what it should be. It would require a ton more work to get right. They prepared their loyal followers for more delays, knowing that this would test their faith yet again. And then, on the 26th of the 4th month of the year 2023, the first glimpse of what one day would become SAGE was released! The founders looked upon it, and saw that it was good. And they named it: Escape Velocity. All was good in the world again, at least for a few days. Now the crowds were even more eagerly looking forward to the gameplay that was promised with the announcement of SAGE. Will ATMTA deliver? And when? Only time will tell…

author: Matt | IntergalacticHerald
content: I don’t think we have a word for what Star Atlas can be. It was a sort of joke for me…saying this will be “more then a game” but the way technology is progressing it is now a core belief in my mind.  We don’t know the future. I hope SA survives and thrives—the Team and the community are unique and the vision is audacious. But anything can happen.  I just want this project to succeed on an emotional level. I want to see what happens when we have a world with the economy and politics of Eve baked in—where RMT is not breaking TOS, it is part of the project. I also want to see what happens when a game engine with the power of UE5 is used to build a vast science fiction based verse. A verse that one day won’t be inhabited by NPCs that stand on tables, but generative agents that make human players wonder if they are interacting with a person who is really into role playing….or an “NPC.”  There’s a possible future for Star Atlas and everyone involved that is so enticing I try not to think about it too much….just because it feels like daydreams brought to life.

reply.preview: I don’t think we have a word for what Star Atlas can be. It was a sort of joke for me…saying this will be “more then a game” but the way technology is progressing it is now a core belief in my mind. We don’t know the future. I hope SA survives and thrives—the Team and the community are unique and the vision is audacious. But anything can happen. I just want this project to succeed on an emotional level. I want to see what happens when we have a world with the economy and politics of Eve baked in—where RMT is not breaking TOS, it is part of the project. I also want to see what happens when a game engine with the power of UE5 is used to build a vast science fiction based verse. A verse that one day won’t be inhabited by NPCs that stand on tables, but generative agents that make human players wonder if they are interacting with a person who is really into role playing….or an “NPC.” There’s a possible future for Star Atlas and everyone involved that is so enticing I try not to think about it too much….just because it feels like daydreams brought to life.

author: odvb
content: Saand is a crew member that you as the player find in a crashed ship. He has amnesia and the story is that you have to restore his memories. Mission wise, you complete quests on zealy, earn XP, and if you earn enough XP, you get his memory fragments which you can turn into prizes either at the end of a mission or at the end of the year. Memory fragments are limited so you will have to choose what you get.  But they're also sellable on the marketplace, so like anythig, you could just buy them at some point

author: JayWalk
content: I read their website. Love the background music and the fact it has numerous factions, not just 3 like pretty much all space games I know, including SA.  They got Hokyo Lim too, which is a major plus. I knew I knew that name from somewhere (if the sentence makes sense).  But to say they can best SA? Hmmm... That's a bit of a stretch. I don't wanna sound like a blind lemming but from what is known, Star Atlas is well ahead of what they care to admit. That's good for the dev phase but bad for the community ("Wen X?" or "We want moar!" factions). Most of what we see here is econ, which is the crucible of the game and all things SA.  So, ofc, the project gets a lot of heat for it (not necessarily unwarranted thought) but what's under the hood is different from what you get from, say Elite Dangerous. >But can you own settlements on ED? >Can you own solar systems in ED? Or colonize systems with no population on them? >Does what you do to/in the economy brings any consequence to you or others?  That I'm aware of, SA is the only one that fills all the checkboxes and more. You can replace Solar Systems with land or whatever counterpart another hypothetical project would have. Again, this is not to say SA is the only project out there with this vision. Just the most high-profile one.

author: ! SH | Bohemian ⪛⦿⫺
content: You are talking about a Space Game compared to a post Apocalyptic game.  Obviously they will have different game aspects.  Kind of a weird comparison.  As of right now you can't do any of that is SA either and no idea of a timeframe when you can.  Same goes for Fidelion.  However what we do know is that Fidelion will have a mobile game coming out in 1-2 months.  Same can't be said for SA.  Those players will be playing Fidelion, upgrading characters / Factions (at some level) and that those players will have time and money invested in that game which will make it harder for them to switch to a new game.  Key takeaway:  There are more and more options for Crypto gamers coming out everyday.  The longer that SA takes to deliver on a game the smaller the pool of players will be as they will be hooked into other games.

author: [AEP] WindSplitter1 [AEP]
content:
There was a development video they released of a feature not seen before. Can't remember which, but you could see the taskbar. And, IIRC, there was a ~5-month gap between the time the video was released vs the time the video was recorded. Meaning, they released something that had been made for longer than that time but chose to drop the alpha much later.

By that, you can extrapolate SA is ahead of the game. More than what they care to admit.

Will concede that I'm tired of hearing about econ and DAO but those are important parts of what the game will be in the future.

author: [AEP] WindSplitter1 [AEP]
content: You are delivering "product", but not a product that any of us want.  We all want a game, and yet we are getting everything except that.  And to rub salt in the wound the team keeps saying how great it is that all of these things are being delivered, yet the appearance is that you have ZERO insight into the Community as it appears tone deaf that you take a victory lap for delivering a marketing tool (NA) to get us working for you yet we keep bleeding money, keep hoping to get something playable and keep wondering when the runway will end.  There were lots of simple solutions to get the Community engaged and playing "something", but the team decided to go down a path that gave us nothing playable instead.  That's the frustration.  That and the approach of not listening to the Community and instead telling us all why we are wrong, rather than listening and accepting why you may be wrong in your approach (and owning it).  I get it...we just have to wait.  We have been waiting for YEARS now for something to play all while other projects are rolling out stuff.  We see what can be possible, yet none of that is coming to fruition here.  Instead we just get that it's coming...  I guess the frustration for me is I see what could have been and compare it to what is...

author: JayWalk
content: As I said, I understand that. Your frustration in that regard is the same for a lot of players/investors as it is mine too. author: ! SH | Bohemian ⪛⦿⫺
content: I think you're being a bit hard on Never Alone. It's not like it took away from any development time. It serves to give the community something for helping us grow. Win/win for both parties. If it's not for you, it's not for you but there isn't any real cons to it tbh if you're concerned about a game (which is what you said). Whether you hate that we say it or not, Never Alone has been a success so far. Not too sure why you would be upset about something working out.  Also, I'm not understanding you saying we don't listen to you guys. Some people say we listen TOO much. We've made plenty of changes based off feedback.   As for the game, taking time. Man. It takes time. We're building a massively ambitious vision at the highest quality possible with bleeding edge technology. Things are coming. We've said this is the year of execution because it is. There's a lot coming out this year.   If you're still disappointed by December 31st 2023, I'll eat a sock. No bullshit.

author: [AEP] WindSplitter1 [AEP]
content: I get that it's ambitious, but it didn't need to be at this point.  You could have had success with just a slick update to SCORE which would have bought you guys a year of happy players, took at most 2-3 month to build and provided tons of ATLAS sinks. Seeing all the other fun Projects rolling out is just making it worse for those of us who have been waiting so long.    Appearance is just that there is misstep after misstep in pivoting all the wrong ways...  Welcome the chance of being proven wrong, but let's be honest here...the track record is not in your favor and our faith has long since been used up.

author: ! SH | Bohemian ⪛⦿⫺
content: I had this conversation with my former colleagues at the BSA last year: There's a roadmap established for the year. If they do not respect it, I'm out. They did and here I am still.  To their defence, they have surprised me with Never Alone as I expected this year to be quite dull which, truth to the matter, it has been.  Note: I'm not saying none of your points are invalid. I just having a different calculus as to where the road here leads to. If I don't like it? Liquidate all assets and move to something else. That's it.

author: ! SH | Bohemian ⪛⦿⫺
content: This is an amazing time for anyone who isn’t technical to leverage a technology that is accelerating at an insane level.  Think of Midjourney as just the surface layer of ai art tech. Download Stable Diffusion. Then go to civitai (.com)  and pick a checkpoint you like and just start copying prompts from the images you vibe with.  Check out Runwayml. gen2 should be out soon. I think the crazy ai videos we will see (like when ai art popped) will be from this project.  Look up Neural Radiance Fields (NeRFs) which will allow anyone to take a pic and make a 3D model. (Think: google earth on crack. Or…make a game based on the interior of your home or even your kitchen table).  Keep an eye (maybe set a google alert) for “GPT-4 UE5 plugin”   Also mess around with Blockade Labs. Prompt a “skybox” (3D world)  Hang out on Huggingface which is the open source community for AI. Many fun projects will be paywalled. But the open source community is large.  Lastly…just hunt around futuretools (.io) for something that catches your eye.  And although you need to link a credit card (once you use up your free tokens from your OpenAI key) you can also download AutoGPT and mess around with an autonomous agent.  I think there are so many tools out there that anyone who wants to build in the Star Atlas universe will find something that inspires them to at least try….

author: [AEP] WindSplitter1 [AEP]
content: This is an amazing time for anyone who isn’t technical to leverage a technology that is accelerating at an insane level.  Think of Midjourney as just the surface layer of ai art tech. Download Stable Diffusion. Then go to civitai (.com)  and pick a checkpoint you like and just start copying prompts from the images you vibe with.  Check out Runwayml. gen2 should be out soon. I think the crazy ai videos we will see (like when ai art popped) will be from this project.  Look up Neural Radiance Fields (NeRFs) which will allow anyone to take a pic and make a 3D model. (Think: google earth on crack. Or…make a game based on the interior of your home or even your kitchen table).  Keep an eye (maybe set a google alert) for “GPT-4 UE5 plugin”   Also mess around with Blockade Labs. Prompt a “skybox” (3D world)  Hang out on Huggingface which is the open source community for AI. Many fun projects will be paywalled. But the open source community is large.  Lastly…just hunt around futuretools (.io) for something that catches your eye.  And although you need to link a credit card (once you use up your free tokens from your OpenAI key) you can also download AutoGPT and mess around with an autonomous agent.  I think there are so many tools out there that anyone who wants to build in the Star Atlas universe will find something that inspires them to at least try….

author: 𝕘𝕣𝕚𝕤𝕥
content:
Reading the Stanford paper about generative agents is worth it…just to get s glimpse of what gaming could be like. It was the impetus for asking the Team what plans they had for AI in this project.

It’s less about GPT-4 being used to make more interesting NPCs and more about a game that will seem like science fiction right now.

Except after you read the paper you realize it’s not that far away.

You will dock on a space station and head off to trade.

Every NPC you encounter will be an Agent. That has a complex relationship with every Agent on the station. They attend birthday parties and hang out with one another.

Every interaction you have will be as if the NPC was a human player who is really into role playing.

Eventually you won’t be able to tell if their neural net was trained on a meat based computer or a silicon chip. 

The paper used a very basic game…it wasn’t about the fidelity of the game….more about how generative Agents behave in a simulated world.

author: ⪛⦿⫺ZeSKK [STAR]
content: What’s scary Noot is last year we were riffing on the future at a nomads stream.  Imagining future waifu stuff. 3D scan of an attractive person, spun up as a Replika chatbot.  What’s hard for me to accept is it was just spitballing. We all hadn’t used ChatGPT yet. We didn’t know about NeRFs. And now that idea is dated as anyone can prompt a person that doesn’t exist and with the right checkpoint really won’t matter that they weren’t a real person.  There’s a global superstar probably being coded right now. And they will burst on the scene and only later will we be told they are AI.

author: [GIA]Strictz9
content: Honestly I got to say that the Never Alone Quest is the most fun I've had in Star Atlas so far.  SCORE was always a chore and not really fun.  The showroom is cool, but still kind of empty until they add more features and I wouldn't call it fun yet.  The racing is mildly fun, but gets repetitive quickly.  This Never Alone Quest is the first time I've really had fun playing something Star Atlas.  The lore is cool, and it's fun following all the quests.  I'm sure SAGE and Showroom will get considerably more enjoyable as they get built out more, but so far Never Alone is the most fun I've had in Star Atlas so far.

author: [GIA]Strictz9
content: I would add that I find lore based activities more interesting than simple racing demos.  Having a story you are working towards revealing adds an emotional incentive to actually do the tasks instead of simply playing demos with no relevance to the game.  Props to whoever designed the Never Alone quest.  I feel emotionally invested to actually learn Saand's full story.  I think adding lore based quests like this to SAGE and Showroom would be a winner as well.

author: Kr1gs
content: Hey @DominicVain I was wondering if there are any official breakdowns of the Solana transaction fees for Escape Velocity? So far I believe the below numbers are correct but im not sure if there are rent accounts. If there are rent accounts how might players close them? Also in future Sage releases who on the team might know how many rent accounts might need to be opened up by new players (again if rent accounts are a thing in Sage)?  Spawn a ship - 0.0040136 SOL Warp 1 - 0.00022932 SOL Warp 2+ - 0.0000066 SOL Process Harvest - 0.0000066 SOL Disband Fleet - 0.00203601 SOL  Ive also been away for a bit so forgive me if any of this has been discussed. I played Escape Velocity for a very short few minutes & tracked my transactions. I created 3 fleets, did 9 Warps, 1 Process Harvest, and disbanded my fleet.  This cost 0.00666216 SOL ($0.1299) in transaction fees. I understand warping becomes much cheaper after the first warp and you do not have to disband your fleet, however im just wondering who might be able to explain to me the path forward for transaction fees while playing future Sage releases? Solana price is very low right now, what happens if the Solana price ever recovers to $260 per token and the transactions noted above cost $1.73. This could kill any future gameplay.  If I have misinterpreted anything please let me know.

Solana price is very low right now, what happens if the Solana price ever recovers to $260 per token and the transactions noted above cost $1.73. This could kill any future gameplay. If I have misinterpreted anything please let me know.

author: [AEP] Funcracker [AEP]
content:
So, wallets were originally never meant to allow users to "sign" anything that is not on-chain. Back in the day what you see happening with Scanning, was how some websites solved the issue where they do want to know you own the wallet, but not make any changes on-chain.

Over time, wallets integrated a way to accommodate this, making it much more clear you are signing an off-chain "message". Star Atlas, however, seems to have chose an alternate route, going old-school. I can not come up with any other explanation than that they need something besides what the current "standard" way of doing off-chain message signing gives them.

author: [AEP] Funcracker [AEP]
content: Perhaps though, we can ask @bravetarget to provide some insight here. Or even dispute my interpretation

author: [AEP] Funcracker [AEP]
content: Hi Funcracker!  You are correct, but the only reason we are using the non-standard approach was to support Ledger devices.  We prefer the signMessage method to verify public key ownership, but the compatibility issues (likely temporary) are what we are working around atm.

reply.preview: Hi Funcracker! You are correct, but the only reason we are using the non-standard approach was to support Ledger devices. We prefer the signMessage method to verify public key ownership, but the compatibility issues (likely temporary) are what we are working around atm.

author: [AEP] WindSplitter1 [AEP]
content: Doesn’t it feel like a superpower?   I’m sure people think this is all hype. It feels like voodoo sometimes seeing the quality that pops up so quickly. I also am a traditional artist. There are so many tools and things to learn it gives me the same joy bringing an idea to life.  My hang up as an artist is trying to make something realistic. I loved “cresting  a picture of an impossible world.” But I never had the talent to match the vision. Using all the tools we have with SD gets me closer to the dream of illustrating imagined worlds. I barley have 1-2 hours of free time and my traditional work took a year to finish. Now…it takes a minute.  Have fun! I can’t wait to see all the great content that will be produced from this community.
